behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is module handoff: each source client -> aggregation/composition layer -> summary input payload. A unit test of a single formatter would miss wiring bugs (e.g., one collector not called or dropped field), but no real OS/network/subprocess is required because deterministic in-process fakes can reproduce the failure artifact.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline up to (but not including) summary generation with three stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel values. Assert the payload sent to the summary step contains all three source sections and their expected mapped fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience logic across modules (source adapter error handling + report assembly). The behavior can be reproduced by making one adapter fail in-process and verifying fallback rendering. Higher levels were considered and rejected: the behavior text only requires graceful degradation, not real network timeout semantics or external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real report orchestration with three source adapters where one adapter raises an availability error and the other two return data. Assert execution completes, output contains a `data unavailable` marker for the failed source section, and other sections are present.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected because no behavior text requires validating OS/network-specific failure mechanics; the required artifact is local fallback composition.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality (faithfulness to numeric/key facts), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated with stubbed responses without hiding real hallucination/distortion failures. Workflow is not required because this behavior targets one generation step, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Using fixed input fixtures with known key values, call the real summary-generation model repeatedly and score outputs against factual checks (key numbers/entities must match input; contradictions fail). Pass criteria are threshold-based across runs to account for variance.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected because stubbed model outputs would not expose factuality failures from real generation.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real external interactions (source APIs and email delivery/inbox reception). In-process fakes can miss transport/auth/delivery-path issues. Agentic was considered, but behavior correctness here is delivery to inbox, not model output quality; summary content can be validated separately.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full script in an environment with reachable test instances of the three data sources and a real email test inbox. Assert end-to-end run success and verify the received email at the configured recipient includes the report payload and expected headers.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected: behavior text requires successful end-to-end delivery, not evaluation of narrative quality.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans external service failure plus cross-run persistence/retry, where real environment behavior matters (unreachable delivery endpoint, process-run boundaries, filesystem persistence). Integration fakes may hide real failure modes in network/IO semantics. No LLM-quality dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute the script against an intentionally unreachable email endpoint and verify it writes the generated report to disk in the expected retry location/format. Run 2 (next scheduled invocation): restore email reachability, execute again, and assert the previously saved report is retried and successfully delivered, then marked cleared/processed.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly includes unreachable external delivery service and next-run persistence semantics that are environment-dependent.
